category_title: Preschoolers with autism exhibiting high unengagement and rigid object manipulation
scenario_title: Passive waiting during caregiving and unstructured activities
minimal_context: The student is finishing a snack and transitioning to free play in the preschool classroom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time for snack. Here are your crackers and juice.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Eats the crackers and drinks the juice, then sits quietly looking at the table, waiting in a passive manner.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you want to do next?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Continues to sit at the table in an unengaged state, not purposefully attending to the remaining food or looking at the teacher.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You can go to the free play area now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Walks to the unstructured play area and stands in the middle of the room, waiting in a passive manner without initiating any functional play acts.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time for snack. Here are your crackers and juice.

STUDENT: (Eats the crackers and drinks the juice, then sits quietly looking at the table, waiting in a passive manner.)

TUTOR: What do you want to do next?

STUDENT: (Continues to sit at the table in an unengaged state, not purposefully attending to the remaining food or looking at the teacher.)

TUTOR: You can go to the free play area now.

STUDENT: (Walks to the unstructured play area and stands in the middle of the room, waiting in a passive manner without initiating any functional play acts.)

context.environment: unstructured (e.g., free play, recess) or caregiving

challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: mostly eating/drinking or waiting in a passive manner

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: spent 37% of the observed time in an unengaged state, where, by deﬁnition, they were not purposefully attending to or interacting with objects or other people
